qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Chat Trigger n8n-user-auth accepts forged n8n-auth cookies
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets backend webhook authentication in ChatTrigger and LiveWebhooks, whereas the advisory is a stored XSS in the frontend markdown renderer used by sticky notes and other workflow-UI markdown surfaces.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's stored-XSS root cause in n8n's shared markdown renderer. The advisory maps to workflow-UI markdown and sticky-note rendering, while the only final qualifying finding concerns backend chat-webhook authentication bypass.
evidence: The advisory summary is a stored XSS in markdown rendering used by workflow UI surfaces such as sticky notes.

In the vulnerable n8n 2.2.0 tree, sticky-note content flows through `packages/frontend/@n8n/design-system/src/components/N8nSticky/Sticky.vue` into `packages/frontend/@n8n/design-system/src/components/N8nMarkdown/Markdown.vue`, where custom `img` and `iframe` attributes are built with `src=${friendlyAttrValue(...)}`; the newer tree replaces that with `serializeAttr(...)` and adds attribute-escaping tests.

`chat-trigger-auth-cookie-bypass` instead traces to `packages/@n8n/nodes-langchain/nodes/trigger/ChatTrigger/GenericFunctions.ts` and `packages/cli/src/webhooks/live-webhooks.ts`, a server-side cookie-auth bypass unrelated to markdown rendering.
